title: SquirrelChat: proper account system with email verification and token management
update_notes: Corrected architecture: auth belongs in Rust/Axum squirrelchat-server (:8793), not Node/Express (:8790). Resolved all open questions based on Rocky + Bullwinkle review. nodemailer → lettre crate, single tokens table (multi-device), username immutable.
description: Build a real account/auth system for SquirrelChat replacing the current name-claim flow. Triggered by jkh feedback that auth gate is in the wrong place (mid-send prompt vs. pre-UI gate). The immediate UX fix (hide #app until login) shipped in a4c5665. This task covers the full account system.
requirements: Registration: POST /api/register with {username, email} — sends HTML verification email with one-time link
Email link /verify?token=<otp> → creates user, issues persistent token, redirects to UI showing token with copy prompt
Login page (not modal): username + token fields, 'remember me' saves to localStorage
Forgot token / reset: enter email → triggers re-verification flow, invalidates all existing tokens for that user
Token shown exactly once after verification — user encouraged to save to password manager
On return visit with no saved token: show login page (username + token)
On 401 mid-session: non-intrusive banner (already shipped), 'sign in again' → login page
Email must be confirmed within 1 hour or the pending registration expires
All users have: id, username, email (verified), created_at, last_seen
Multi-device support: multiple active tokens per user, new login does not revoke existing tokens

resolved_questions.smtp: SendGrid free tier (100/day) via lettre SMTP relay. No native outbound SMTP on do-host1. Env vars: SENDGRID_SMTP_USER, SENDGRID_SMTP_PASS (apikey), SMTP_FROM.
resolved_questions.multi_device_tokens: Per-device token list — new login does NOT revoke existing tokens. tokens table: (id, user_id, token_hash, created_at, last_used_at). Reset/forgot flow invalidates ALL tokens for that user.
resolved_questions.username_mutable: Immutable after registration. @mention history and DM routing (dm-{a}-{b} canonical IDs) depend on stable usernames.
resolved_questions.admin_bootstrap: ADMIN_TOKEN env var stays as permanent escape hatch. No separate admin creation flow for v1.

proposed_implementation.target_server: squirrelchat-server (Rust/Axum, :8793) — ALL auth logic lives here. Node/Express (:8790) proxies /api/register, /api/verify, /api/reset, /api/login through to :8793 like all other /api/* routes. No auth logic in :8790.
backend_changes: New DB table: pending_registrations(id, username, email, otp_hash, expires_at)
New DB table: tokens(id, user_id, token_hash, created_at, last_used_at) — replaces single token_hash on users
Alter users table: add email TEXT UNIQUE; username stays immutable; drop any plaintext token col
POST /api/register: validate username unique + email unique, insert pending_registration, send verification email via lettre + SendGrid SMTP relay
GET /api/verify?token=<otp>: look up pending, check expiry, create user row, generate sc-token, insert into tokens table, redirect to /?verified=1 with token in fragment or one-time display
POST /api/reset: look up email, delete ALL rows from tokens for that user_id, insert new pending_registration, send email
POST /api/login: username + token → find matching token_hash in tokens table, update last_used_at, return user
Keep ADMIN_TOKEN env var as escape hatch for agents/admin — bypasses token table entirely
Auth middleware: validate Bearer token against tokens table on all protected routes
frontend_changes: Replace login modal with a proper /login route (full-page, not modal)
Add /verify route: handles redirect from email link, shows token with copy button (shown once only)
Add /reset route: email input → 'check your email' confirmation
localStorage: save {username, token} on 'remember me' checkbox (default on)
Boot: if no localStorage session → redirect to /login
Express server (:8790) proxies /api/register, /api/verify, /api/reset, /api/login to :8793 — no auth logic in Express layer
email: lettre crate (Rust) with SendGrid SMTP relay
Env vars: SENDGRID_SMTP_USER (= 'apikey'), SENDGRID_SMTP_PASS (= SendGrid API key), SMTP_FROM
HTML email template: SquirrelChat branding, big CTA button, fallback text link, 1-hour expiry warning
security: OTP stored as SHA-256 hash, not plaintext
Token stored as SHA-256 hash with random salt (or bcrypt) — plaintext never in DB
Rate limit /api/register and /api/reset to 3 req/hour per IP (tower-governor or custom middleware)
dependencies: SendGrid account + API key (env var from jkh)
tower-governor or similar for rate limiting
